Excessive intake of alcohol suppresses appetite and as a result, leads to several nutritional deficiency diseases. People who regularly consume more than two alcoholic drinks (one equals about 30 ml of ethanol) are at a higher risk for hypertension and stroke. Alcohol intake has also been shown to increase the risk of cancer of the mouth, larynx and oesophagus, prostate and of the breast in women. Excessive alcohol intake weakens the heart muscle (cardiomyopathy) and also damages the liver (cirrhosis), brain and peripheral nerves.
